summary: Official Windows service control for Windows Search (WSearch). Microsoft documents the service name, default start mode, and says it is OK to disable on Windows IoT Enterprise. The app disables the service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

setting.name: Windows Search Service
setting.casual_explanation: Windows Search builds and maintains an index so file, email, and content searches can return results faster. Disabling it reduces indexing activity, but it also removes the indexed search experience.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they do not use Windows indexing, prefer third-party search tools, or want to reduce indexing-related background work.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning off the service can reduce indexing work, but Windows Search features lose indexed results and some experiences can become slower or unavailable.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft's Windows IoT Enterprise service guidance lists Windows Search `(WSearch)` with a default start mode of `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`. Microsoft also documents the Windows Search indexing process and notes that the service provides content indexing, property caching, and search results for files, email, and other content. The app uses a service start-mode tweak that disables `WSearch`.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Windows Search runs on demand with the documented default start mode.

allowed_values.label: Search service disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Indexed Windows Search is unavailable until the service start mode is restored.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app disables the WSearch service by setting its start mode to Disabled.

windows_defaults.label: Microsoft documented default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows systems using the normal Windows Search service configuration

states.rationale: Microsoft lists WSearch with a Manual default start mode in the service guidance.

windows_defaults.notes: This baseline preserves indexed Windows Search behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Anyone relying on Start or Explorer search
avoid_for: Users who intentionally do not want indexing

states.rationale: Keep the documented default service mode.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You use Windows Search and want the normal indexed search experience.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You prefer third-party search tools and want indexing off.
tradeoffs: The service can still do indexing work when needed.

recommended_profiles.label: No search indexing
intended_for: Users who do not rely on Windows Search
Systems using Everything or other external search tools
avoid_for: Users who rely on indexed file or mail search
Systems where Search features must remain available

states.rationale: Disable the service to remove indexed search behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally do not want the Windows Search indexing service running.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want fast indexed search results in Windows.
tradeoffs: Windows Search features lose the indexed search experience.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Guidance on configuring system services - Windows Search
evidence.summary: Microsoft lists Windows Search `(WSearch)` with start mode `Manual` and marks it `OK to disable`, describing it as providing content indexing, property caching, and search results.

evidence.title: Local SCM snapshot - WSearch
evidence.summary: Checked-in `sc qc WSearch` snapshot from Microsoft Windows 11 Pro 10.0.26200 confirming the exact service identifier, display name, binary path, and current START_TYPE line on the review host. This proves the exact SCM control surface the app targets, but it does not replace Microsoft's default-mode guidance.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Search indexing process overview
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the Windows Search indexing system and confirms the feature area and its role in indexed search results.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider disables the `WSearch` service through the batch service start-mode tweak.

decision.why: Microsoft documents Windows Search `(WSearch)` with a Manual default start mode, marks it `OK to disable`, and states that it provides content indexing, property caching, and search results. The app disables the same documented service control surface by setting its start mode to Disabled.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Windows Search (WSearch) | Manual | OK to disable | Provides content indexing, property caching, and search results for files, e-mail, and other content.
validation_proof.notes: The official Learn services page documents the exact WSearch service name, default start mode, disable guidance, and behavior. A companion SCM snapshot is checked in at Docs/tweaks/research/notes/service-snapshots/wsearch-sc-qc-2026-03-14.txt.
